old_claim.note: Plague Pesthouses provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The 1423 hospital for infected patients was the institutional predecessor to Venice's distinct 1468 station for exposed people and cargo.

source_shape.source_locator: History section: distinguishes the 1423 hospital for infected patients from the 1468 Lazzaretto Nuovo, where potentially infected people and goods underwent quarantine.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Venice added Lazzaretto Nuovo in 1468 to quarantine potentially infected travelers and goods, distinct from its 1423 plague hospital.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The chronology supports predecessor lineage rather than a one-to-one enabling dependency. The 1423 treatment hospital preceded but did not technically enable the distinct 1468 preventive station for exposed people and cargo.

ontology_before: plague_pesthouses was modeled as an enabling capability for quarantine_lazarettos.
ontology_after: plague_pesthouses is retained as historical lineage for quarantine_lazarettos, not as a necessary implementation capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: quarantine_lazarettos->plague_pesthouses is historical_predecessor.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that the scoped quarantine_lazarettos implementation technically requires plague_pesthouses.
short_reason: The 1423 treatment hospital preceded but did not technically enable the distinct 1468 preventive station for exposed people and cargo.
